summary: Research session start. PRD, field notes, and product notes exist. No presearch criteria, decisions, or plan yet.

agent_asked: Is MVP one-bottle-at-a-time or batch/multi-bottle photo?
human_said: Tiered: T1=single photo, T2=shelf photo multi-ID, T3=video. Each tier must work before expanding.

agent_asked: Which VLM for identification? Cost ceiling per image?
human_said: Gemini 2.5 Flash free tier to start, expand based on testing findings.

query: Gemini 2.5 Flash free tier rate limits vision capabilities 2026
finding: Free: 10RPM, 250req/day, 250K TPM. Paid: $0.30/1M in, $2.50/1M out. Supports image input + structured JSON. Zero-shot object detection with bboxes. Bug in flash-image variant with JSON mode.

agent_asked: Thin proxy or stateful backend? Persistence in Tier 1?

query: Hono web framework Bun 2026 file uploads multipart ORM
finding: Hono+Bun production-ready. Multipart via parseBody(), bodyLimit middleware. Drizzle ORM best pairing. 99k req/s. Runtime-agnostic.

agent_asked: What database? Where to store images?
human_said: Give tradeoffs of Postgres+images-in-DB vs S3. Defer decision until team meeting — currently focused on mobile app scaffold.

rationale: User focused on mobile scaffold, will decide with team. Tradeoffs presented.

agent_asked: Expo vs bare RN? Navigation? RN experience level?
human_said: Expo + Expo Router. 4+ years RN/Expo experience.
Batch photos to cut costs, support batch of 1. ScrollView populates as backend returns results progressively.

agent_asked: SSE vs WebSocket vs polling for progressive results? Camera vs photo library?
human_said: Explain SSE. Give WS tradeoffs. Support both camera and library. Stretch: option to enforce same-day photo metadata.

query: SSE vs WebSocket for batch processing results in Hono+RN
finding: SSE simpler fit for one-way batch result push. RN needs polyfill. WebSocket built into RN but adds complexity. SSE for Tier 1, WebSocket if bidirectional needed later.

agent_asked: What viz for graph screen? MVP vs stretch?
human_said: MVP: current inventory snapshot + reorder alerts. Stretch: cost estimation, historical trends.

agent_asked: Auth, data model, reorder logic, export, team structure, deployment?
human_said: Auth: seeded UN/PW. Data model: brand, release/type, vintage, UPC/GTIN-12, volume. Reorder: alert when <1/4 full and no backup in inventory; stretch: slow-seller flag. Export: MVP. Team: Ray=mobile, Jacob=backend+VLM+GTM, Patrick=bottle DB/UPC research+web mgmt (post-MVP). Deploy: free/low-cost hosting with custom domain.

agent_asked: Draft API contract? Tab-based or linear navigation?
human_said: 1. Added preliminary Drizzle data model in docs/prelim_data_model.ts. 2. Tab-based navigation.

rationale: Tabs for Capture, Inventory, Alerts, Settings (or similar)

finding: Good separation: bottles (catalog) vs inventory (bar instance) vs scans (photo events). Questions: fill level enum vs numeric slider, vintage missing, no session/batch grouping concept, bottles as global catalog confirmation needed.

rationale: VLM gives coarse estimates, user slider gives fine granularity. User corrections double as SME-annotated training data.
Assumption: shared bottle DB that backend references for all users. Patrick researching.
Groups scans for batch SSE streaming, review UX, and historical tracking. Added with status enum, counts, timestamps, indexes.

finding: No free comprehensive bottle DB. Best free sources: TTB COLA Registry (label images) + Iowa Liquor Products (UPC+pricing, CC BY 4.0). GTIN-12/UPC-A as canonical key confirmed. Multi-signal matching (barcode→OCR→embeddings→HITL) is the production path. OFF has ODbL share-alike licensing risk. Commercial APIs $75-700/mo.

query: Analyze new files from git pull
finding: Major updates: 1) Schema rewritten — bars→venues, locations table, fillLevelTenths (0-10), expanded categories (spirits+consumables), inventory keyed on (location,bottle). 2) Eval harness uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 via Agent SDK + LangSmith, not Gemini. 3) Dashboard scaffolded with Mantine, PAR-based reorder logic. 4) Postgres+Drizzle confirmed. 5) Verbena real bar data (~397 products) as fixture/eval ground truth.

human_said: Schema rewritten by Jacob — fill level is now single integer 0-10 tenths, not dual enum+int. Training data annotation is implicit (vlmFillTenths on scan vs fillLevelTenths on inventory).
resolution: Updated presearch_criteria.md and domain-map.md to reflect new schema.

human_said: Eval harness uses Claude Sonnet 4.6, not Gemini 2.5 Flash. VLM choice now TBD pending eval results.
resolution: Updated presearch_criteria.md criterion 6 to reflect model uncertainty.

human_said: Reorder logic changed to PAR-based thresholds (from dashboard specs), not simple <25%+no backup. backstockQuantity removed; backup derived from cross-location inventory.

rationale: Leverages existing Claude Code subscription — no separate API costs. Gemini as fallback if needed. Model swappable via DEFAULT_MODEL in types.ts.

summary: Scope locked. MVP: mobile capture (Expo), backend (Hono+Bun+Postgres+Drizzle), dashboard (Mantine), VLM (Claude Sonnet 4.6), PAR-based reorder, CSV export. 13/16 presearch criteria answered. 3 deferred (failure handling, security, deployment).

coverage: all MVP features mapped — capture, VLM, review, inventory, alerts, export, auth, dashboard integration
